exit_criteria: Accepted UI redesign requirements are represented in owning UI knowledge docs.
Documentation build maps feedback build to changed knowledge paths and implementation planning handoff.
Planning/implementation start from documentation build rather than chat memory.

loop_start.reason: Feedback build required documentation loop to start from artifact, not prior chat.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Documentation changes should be validated against accepted feedback before planning/implementation consumes them.

next_loop.reason: Planning and implementation should consume this documentation build and docs, not chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Simplify user-facing UI language and avoid backend jargon where possible.
Move top-level navigation into the header and remove left rail, right inspector/sidebar, and bottom status line.
Remove repeated section titles and one-line explanatory subtitles from default section chrome; use breadcrumbs/context only when helpful.
Rename Product/System top-level navigation into a single KB section with Product and System drilldown.
Within Product KB, expose Stories and UI surfaces as fast selectable lists with ASCII-like detail cards; users/personas remain contextual evidence.
Within System KB, use ASCII-like diagrams/cards and fix stacked/overlapping diagram rendering; component interactions should open detail cards.
Board should remain a Trello-like Kanban board while visually coherent with the ASCII-like design.
Rename Graph to Map and default it to documentation/KB relationship visibility; broader graph data should be secondary.
Rename Claims to Sessions in the user UI and focus on active sessions rather than claim internals.
Settings should represent repo-backed user preferences, not a visible path map to .codewiki/config.json; source paths may be advanced detail only.
Header should remove local-first wording and show only the local URL at top right with the settings icon.
The UI is a secondary-screen observability surface used alongside agent chat, not a terminal replacement.

summary: Documented accepted ASCII-like CodeWiki UI redesign: header navigation, KB consolidation, Map/Sessions terminology, inline cards, repo-backed settings, and secondary-screen constraints.
knowledge_changes: Replaced Control Room left-rail model with header nav: Status, KB, Board, Map, Sessions.
Documented no persistent sidebars/status line and inline ASCII-like detail cards with collapsed source disclosure.
Consolidated Product/System into KB with Product/System drilldown and Product default lists for Stories/UI Surfaces.
Renamed Graph UI to Map and Claims UI to Sessions in product/system docs.
Documented repo-backed preferences via header settings cog and local URL-only header detail.
Updated Board and Status Panel docs to match ASCII-like layout and terminology.
roadmap_changes: Create/update implementation task for ASCII-like CodeWiki UI redesign using this documentation build.
open_questions: Which repo config keys should become editable preferences first?
Should the first Map doc relationship view derive only from KB docs or also include tasks/build evidence that link docs?
